content.claim: Hungary is an OECD outlier on socioeconomic performance gradients, with children's economic, social and cultural status explaining over 20% of variance in reading, science and mathematics (compared to OECD average of 12-16%), and disadvantaged students having less than one-in-eight chance of attending school with high achievers.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: PISA measures 15-year-olds already sorted into tracks, cannot isolate causal share specific to 6/8-year gimnázium mechanism
Reflects broader 3-way secondary tracking (gimnázium/szakgimnázium/szakképző) not solely early selection
Cross-sectional measurement does not establish causation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
